target.title: fix: resolve managed SecretRef provider auth
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92097.

This teaches provider auth resolution to bridge managed/non-env SecretRef provider `apiKey` entries through the active runtime config snapshot. The source config can keep the managed SecretRef marker/object, while runtime auth uses the already-resolved literal key from the matching runtime snapshot.

The change is intentionally narrow:

- only applies when the source provider entry has a managed non-env SecretRef marker/object
- only trusts a runtime snapshot when it matches the active source snapshot
- does not make marker-only source config work without a runtime snapshot
- keeps explicit provider api-key config ahead of ambient profile/env auth
- blocks plugin synthetic local auth from shadowing an explicit managed provider SecretRef

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/model-auth.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` — 64 passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` — clean, overall patch correct
- `git diff --check -- src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json` — core and coreTests only

Live E2E/proof from Crabbox on this branch before the final synthetic-shadowing regression hardening:

- provider: `aws`
- lease: `cbx_4b1a7511118d`
- run: `run_57dd3b102653`
- positive SecretRef-backed custom provider reached fake upstream with Authorization present
- marker-only source config failed before upstream auth, preserving the intended invalid-config behavior

Known proof gap: `pnpm check:changed` delegated to Blacksmith Testbox but remained queued; I stopped our queued run `tbx_01ktvv2bwe5y5psshpae92fj2w` rather than leaving it pending.
